title: Second Dissociation Constant of Phosphoric Acid : A Recalculation from Alkaline Solutions Using  Modified Davies Equation
author: A. K. GHOSH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Patna University, Patna 

  Manuscript received 21 March 1978, revised 25 February 1979, accepted 7 March 1979  

 The second dissociation constant of phosphoric acid in alkaline solution has been recalculated from the e.m.f. data of Bates and Acree from 0° to 50° in steps of 5°, using modified Davies equation. At each temperature the recalculated value of pK 2  closely agrees with the pK 2  value given by Bates and Acree. 
Our method of determining pK value of acids is accurate as well as simpler.
